data.title: AquaSculpt Reviews (INVESTIGATED): Shocking Ice Water Hack or Strategic Weight Loss Breakthrough?
data.description:
A Deep Dive Into the Ice-Triggered Fat Loss Formula That's Turning Heads in 2025: What Users, Science, and Consumer Reports Are Now Revealing About AquaSculpt

DENVER, PA / ACCESS Newswire / July 19, 2025 / If you've been hearing whispers about a so-called "ice water hack" that promises to burn fat by optimizing internal temperature and metabolism, you're not alone. AquaSculpt has exploded into the spotlight in 2025 as one of the most unconventional, and widely discussed, fat loss supplements on the market. Marketed as a metabolism-supporting Formula Inspired By Cold Exposure Therapy, AquaSculpt claims to activate thermogenesis without stimulants or crash diets.

But the bigger question remains: Does AquaSculpt actually work? Or is this just another supplement trending on hype and questionable marketing tactics?

In this full investigative report, we'll uncover what makes AquaSculpt different, what science says about its key ingredients, and Whether The Thousands Of Consumer Reviews And "Before And After" Photos Really Live Up To The Buzz (Source). You'll also learn the truth about AquaSculpt complaints, refund policies, and insider warnings about fake listings online.

Let's dig in, because before you buy any bottle of AquaSculpt, this is what you need to know.

>> See The Verified Results And Case Studies Shared Directly On The Official AquaSculpt Site

What Is AquaSculpt and Why Is Everyone Suddenly Talking About It in 2025?

AquaSculpt is quickly becoming one of the most talked-about fat-burning solutions in 2025-and not just because of slick marketing. This supplement taps into an increasingly popular metabolic concept: activating your body's natural thermogenesis using principles inspired by cold exposure therapy. But rather than sitting in an ice bath every morning, AquaSculpt claims to deliver similar metabolic activation through a carefully engineered daily capsule.

So what exactly is AquaSculpt? It's marketed as a non-stimulant, plant-based supplement designed to enhance the body's ability to burn fat by mimicking the effects of cold exposure at a cellular level. The formula includes a blend of natural compounds that support brown fat activation, regulate internal temperature, and promote metabolic efficiency, all while avoiding caffeine, jittery side effects, or sleep disruption.

The Rise In Aquasculpt Reviews Isn't A Coincidence (Verified). Consumers across the U.S. and Canada have been searching for fat-burning alternatives that don't rely on aggressive stimulants or unsustainable dieting. As a result, AquaSculpt has carved out a new niche by offering what many believe is a smarter, safer, and more natural approach to metabolic enhancement.

A key reason AquaSculpt is trending in health circles and beyond? Its positioning around the "ice water hack." Social media videos and YouTube channels have showcased the idea that cold exposure, whether through ice baths, cold showers, or now, AquaSculpt capsules, will activate dormant fat-burning mechanisms. While the research is still emerging, early studies on cold-triggered thermogenesis and brown adipose tissue (BAT) Confirm There's Legitimate Scientific Potential Behind The Concept.

In short, AquaSculpt isn't just riding the wave of wellness trends-it's tapping into a physiological process that many experts believe could be a breakthrough in sustainable fat loss. And with tens of thousands of bottles sold, and the phrase "AquaSculpt reviews" trending across platforms, there's no doubt this supplement has hit a nerve with consumers in 2025.

>>Discover Why AquaSculpt's Cold-Trigger Formula Is Dominating Fat Burner Reviews

AquaSculpt Reviews From Verified Users: Real Transformations or Just Marketing?

There's a reason search terms like "AquaSculpt reviews" and "real AquaSculpt before and after" are spiking on Google. Prospective users want proof. Are these glowing AquaSculpt testimonials actually grounded in real results, or is this just another hype-fueled supplement bubble?

According To A Large Pool Of Verified Buyers Across Review Aggregators and third-party platforms, AquaSculpt appears to be delivering noticeable results for a wide range of users. The most frequently reported outcomes include improved fat loss in stubborn areas, reduced cravings, stable energy without crashes, and less bloating-especially within the first 3 to 4 weeks of consistent use.

Here's what a few verified users had to say:

"I was skeptical at first, especially with all the buzz around the ice water hack. But AquaSculpt surprised me. I didn't drop 10 pounds overnight-but I did feel lighter, less bloated, and more energized by day 10." - Rachel G., age 43

"I've tried every fat burner on the market. AquaSculpt is the first that didn't mess with my sleep or leave me shaky. I dropped 7 pounds in 3 weeks without changing much-just taking it each morning with cold water." - Don L., age 51

>>See If AquaSculpt Lives Up To The Hype, Read What Real Users Have Shared

Still, it's not all five-star ratings. A small percentage of AquaSculpt complaints cite minor digestive issues in the first few days, often linked to sensitive stomachs. Others mentioned that results took longer than expected-usually due to inconsistent use or unrealistic expectations for rapid weight loss.

Bottom line: AquaSculpt reviews suggest the supplement works best when paired with consistency, realistic expectations, and a basic understanding of how fat loss happens over time-not instantly.

How AquaSculpt Works: The Cold Exposure Trigger Explained Scientifically

What sets AquaSculpt apart is its unique mechanism of action. While most fat-burning supplements rely on stimulants like caffeine to artificially raise your metabolic rate, AquaSculpt aims to activate a more natural and sustainable fat-burning process known as non-shivering thermogenesis-the body's ability to generate heat and burn calories in response to cold.

The secret lies in brown adipose tissue (BAT), a specialized form of body fat that burns energy to create warmth. Unlike white fat, which stores calories, brown fat is metabolically active. When BAT is stimulated, it can increase calorie expenditure even at rest. Scientific studies have shown that cold exposure-like jumping into a cold shower or sitting in an ice bath-can activate brown fat and kickstart this thermogenic process.

>>Visit The Official AquaSculpt Website To See Why Thousands Are Making The Switch In 2025

AquaSculpt claims to mimic this cold exposure effect by using ingredients that signal the body to initiate thermogenesis and enhance mitochondrial efficiency. While the exact proprietary formula remains under wraps, key components include natural adaptogens, thermogenic botanicals, and metabolic regulators that are believed to support BAT activity.

In plain terms, AquaSculpt works by tricking your body into thinking it's in a cold environment-without the need to actually dunk yourself into freezing water. This approach allows the body to burn more calories throughout the day, supporting fat loss over time while maintaining steady energy levels.

And because AquaSculpt doesn't rely on stimulants, it may be suitable for individuals sensitive to caffeine or looking for a fat-loss strategy that supports hormonal balance, sleep, and sustainable metabolic health.

The science is still developing, but The Medical Research Behind Aquasculpt Is Grounded In Peer-Reviewed Research On Cold-Induced Thermogenesis And Brown Fat Activation. Combine that with real-world user feedback, and it's clear why AquaSculpt reviews continue to gain momentum among those seeking smarter, stimulant-free fat-burning solutions.

>>Ready To Experience Real Results? AquaSculpt Offers A 180-Day Money-Back Promise.

This part is critical. Many of the AquaSculpt complaints and refund issues reported in 2025 stem from one simple mistake: buying from the wrong place.

Despite AquaSculpt's growing popularity, it's not sold in stores, Walmart, CVS, or GNC, and while it may appear on Amazon, that doesn't mean it's safe to buy there.

The Official Website Is the Only Verified Source

AquaSculpt's creators have confirmed that the Only Legitimate Purchase Source Is Their Official Website. Buying from anywhere else could result in:

  • Expired formulas or improperly stored capsules

  • Counterfeit bottles that look identical but contain different contents

  • Voided refund rights and zero customer support

The 180-day money-back guarantee only applies to purchases made directly through the authorized website. This means if you buy AquaSculpt on Amazon or eBay and something goes wrong? You're likely out of luck.

Why Amazon Can't Be Trusted for AquaSculpt

While Amazon is convenient, it operates as an open marketplace, which means any seller can list any product, including fakes. Some buyers in early 2025 unknowingly purchased AquaSculpt through Amazon sellers who:

  • Shipped different capsules entirely

  • Didn't honor the 180-day refund window

>> Take Advantage Of The 73% Off Offer Before It's Gone From The Official AquaSculpt Site

Even worse? Some of these listings were never removed. That's why the only safe way to buy AquaSculpt is directly from the brand. It ensures:

If you're serious about trying AquaSculpt, don't let convenience sabotage your results. The official site offers Bulk Discounts, Free Shipping, And Even Free Bonus Books (Source) for eligible bundles-something no Amazon listing can guarantee.

AquaSculpt Complaints in 2025: Mild Reactions or Bigger Red Flags?

With any supplement that explodes in popularity, online complaints inevitably follow. But when it comes to AquaSculpt complaints in 2025, the key is understanding the context-and the sources.

Let's address the biggest issues head-on:

1. Digestive Sensitivity (Reported in <3% of Cases)

Some AquaSculpt reviews mention mild bloating or cramping in the first few days. This is largely attributed to the prebiotic fiber content, like Inulin, which temporarily alters gut flora as your body adjusts. In nearly all cases, these symptoms resolve within 72 hours and can be minimized by taking AquaSculpt with a full glass of water and a light meal.

Crucially,There Are No Reports Of Serious Side Effects, such as heart palpitations, nausea, or dependency-problems often linked to other fat loss products.

2. Shipping Delay Misconceptions

One of the more common AquaSculpt complaints in forums isn't about the product-but about shipping speed. Here's the truth:

  • Orders placed directly through the official AquaSculpt website are processed within 24 hours and shipped via tracked courier.

  • Delays often occur when users purchase through unauthorized third-party sellers, who sometimes resell outdated or fake product stock.

This confusion has led to unfair reviews blaming AquaSculpt for slow delivery when the real issue was buying from non-official sources like shady Amazon listings or fake Shopify clones.

3. Counterfeit Bottles and Scam Sites

Another rising complaint trend? Counterfeit AquaSculpt bottles. These often look identical but contain untested fillers or expired formulas. Customers who bought these fakes sometimes leave negative reviews about lack of results or side effects-not realizing they never took the real thing.

To avoid this, Only Purchase Aquasculpt From The Verified Official Website. This guarantees:

  • Authentic, updated formulation

  • Active 180-day refund policy

  • Reliable U.S. customer support

So are the AquaSculpt complaints legitimate? Mostly not. The real AquaSculpt performs consistently well-especially when sourced from the right place and used consistently. Most reported issues stem from external factors, not the formula itself.

AquaSculpt Consumer Reports: What Verified Buyers Are Really Saying Now

If you've been digging through AquaSculpt reviews online and wondering whether any of it is actually legit, you're not alone. That's why we went straight to real AquaSculpt Consumer Reports and verified buyer testimonials to see what users are actually experiencing-not just what marketing pages promise.

The verdict? The feedback is louder than ever in 2025, and in many cases, shockingly positive.

Verified AquaSculpt Reviews: What Users Are Actually Reporting

Buyers who've gone through the full 30-, 60-, or 90-day AquaSculpt experience consistently report benefits that go far beyond basic bloating relief. While some started using AquaSculpt for mild weight loss, they ended up experiencing:

  • Noticeable loss of stubborn belly fat (especially around the waistline)

  • Easier digestion and reduced bloating after meals

  • Improved mood, reduced stress cravings, and lower nighttime snacking

Many testimonials from AquaSculpt consumer reports mention a steady transformation-not an overnight miracle, but a real shift in body composition, food cravings, and overall wellness.

AquaSculpt User Timelines: 7 to 90 Days

In the first 7 days, users often describe AquaSculpt as surprisingly gentle, with reduced bloating and water retention being the first visible signs.

By 30 days, the conversation shifts to fat loss, less mid-day fatigue, and higher energy-without any stimulants.

By 90 days, a large number of AquaSculpt buyers say they've either hit their weight goal or are well on their way, with no rebound effect or dependency.

This is critical because unlike aggressive fat burners that deliver short-term drops in water weight, AquaSculpt works on your metabolism, hormone balance, and gut health-all of which contribute to long-term, sustainable results.

AquaSculpt Consumer Reports vs. Paid Reviews: Know the Difference

AquaSculpt has built such a name that fake review sites and promotional blogs have started popping up to hijack traffic. That's why it's important to verify the source of reviews. Real consumer feedback often includes:

The fake ones? You'll notice vague language, no dates, and photos pulled from stock image banks.

Consumer Reports-style feedback posted in trustworthy forums and eCommerce reviews confirms one thing: Aquasculpt Works-But Only If You Stick With It And Buy The Real Version.

AquaSculpt Ingredients Breakdown: What's Inside and What It's Supposed to Do

When it comes to any supplement promising fat loss, the first question savvy shoppers ask is: what's actually inside it? In the case of AquaSculpt, the ingredient list is one of its strongest selling points-particularly because it avoids the usual suspects like caffeine, synephrine, or other synthetic stimulants that dominate traditional fat burners.

AquaSculpt's formula is rooted in non-stimulant thermogenic science, which means it works with your body's natural metabolic triggers rather than forcing it into overdrive. The core strategy behind AquaSculpt is to mimic cold exposure's metabolic effects, activating brown fat and thermogenesis without relying on risky chemical accelerants.

Though the exact proprietary blend is protected, confirmed AquaSculpt ingredients include a combination of adaptogens, minerals, and Plant-Based Thermogenics That Support Mitochondrial Function, Metabolic Regulation, and blood sugar control:

  • Chromium Picolinate: Included for its role in improving insulin sensitivity and helping regulate appetite and sugar metabolism-crucial for fat storage reduction.

  • Berberine HCL: Known for activating AMPK, the cellular "fat switch," this compound supports thermogenesis and may enhance how cells use glucose and fat for energy.

  • Fucoxanthin (from brown seaweed extract): This rare carotenoid has been shown in animal and preliminary human trials to help stimulate brown fat and increase resting metabolic rate.

  • Inulin & Chicory Root Fiber: These prebiotic fibers support digestion and gut flora, which in turn plays a role in appetite control and inflammation reduction.

  • Olive Leaf Extract: Rich in oleuropein, which supports cardiovascular health and metabolic flexibility.

Notably, AquaSculpt's ingredients are entirely natural, and every capsule is manufactured in a GMP-certified, FDA-registered U.S. facility. There are no fillers, artificial colors, or hidden synthetic ingredients, which makes it highly appealing to health-conscious users looking for clean supplementation.

Unlike other fat loss products that hide behind exotic-sounding blends with no real science, AquaSculpt's formulation reflects a deliberate choice: to create synergy between natural cold-triggered metabolic support and cellular energy optimization. It's this harmony that explains why AquaSculpt reviews consistently highlight both fast results and ease of use.

>> Don't Fall For Imitation Bottles, Visit The Only Trusted Source For AquaSculpt

Is AquaSculpt Safe to Take Daily? Medical Experts Weigh In on Side Effects

One of the first things people search alongside "AquaSculpt reviews" is: Is AquaSculpt actually safe? Given the thousands of fat-burning supplements that have come and gone-many pulled off the market for harsh side effects-this is a fair concern.

The good news? AquaSculpt is 100% stimulant-free and designed specifically for daily, long-term use. It contains no caffeine, no synthetic thermogenics, and no ingredients flagged by the FDA. That alone gives it a strong safety profile compared to traditional fat burners.

According to the brand and independent third-party testing, AquaSculpt is manufactured in GMP-certified, FDA-registered labs inside the U.S., using only ingredients that meet stringent purity and potency standards. That means each capsule is tested not just for what it includes-but for what it doesn't (like heavy metals, mold, or banned compounds).

So, what are the side effects, if any?

Based on verified AquaSculpt user reviews and medical commentary, the most common side effects are extremely mild and typically short-lived. A small percentage of users (under 5%) report digestive adjustment issues-such as bloating or mild cramping-in the first few days of taking AquaSculpt, often due to the introduction of prebiotic fibers or detoxifying ingredients.

Crucially, no reports of elevated heart rate, jitteriness, or sleep disruption have surfaced from legitimate buyers using the real product (purchased through the official AquaSculpt website). These issues are more commonly tied to stimulant-based competitors or knockoffs found on shady third-party marketplaces.

Medical experts who've reviewed AquaSculpt's formulation agree: it's safe for daily use among adults over 18, especially those seeking metabolic support without the tradeoff of overstimulation. That makes it especially appealing for women over 40, busy professionals, and anyone sensitive to caffeine or harsh ingredients.

In short, if safety is a top priority in your supplement choices, AquaSculpt delivers exactly that. With consistent dosing and hydration, most users find it not only effective, but incredibly gentle on the system.

Who Should Use AquaSculpt - And Who May Want to Skip It?

Not every supplement is right for every person-but when it comes to AquaSculpt, the appeal is surprisingly broad. Whether you're a 45-year-old parent struggling with stubborn belly fat, a 60-year-old aiming to reset your metabolism post-menopause, or a younger adult trying to avoid stimulant overload, AquaSculpt has positioned itself as a universal solution grounded in metabolic balance and real fat loss science.

The ideal AquaSculpt user is someone who:

  • Wants to burn fat without relying on caffeine, crash diets, or extreme exercise regimens

  • Prefers natural, plant-based supplementation without synthetic additives

  • Has hit a weight loss plateau and is looking to reactivate a sluggish metabolism

  • Is age 35+ and noticing fat storage in new areas due to hormonal shifts or aging

  • Is trying to lose fat without the harsh crash or stress of traditional fat burners

In short, AquaSculpt is for people who want to lose weight the smarter, safer way-not the fastest or most extreme. It's especially attractive to mid-life users who care about long-term metabolic health and hormone balance, not just the number on the scale.

That said, AquaSculpt may not be ideal for:

  • Anyone under 18

  • Pregnant or breastfeeding women

  • Those with a history of very low blood sugar or current medical restrictions (always consult a doctor if unsure)

  • People expecting overnight or dramatic weight drops in just a few days-AquaSculpt is designed for steady, natural progress, not miracle results

For those who fit the right profile and want to activate their body's dormant fat-burning mechanisms without stimulants, AquaSculpt may be one of the most compelling formulas released in 2025.

>>Curious If This Ice Hack Could Work For You? The Official Website Explains It All

AquaSculpt Results Timeline: What to Expect After 7, 30, and 60 Days

One of the most common questions asked in AquaSculpt reviews is simple: "How long does it take to see results?" While every individual responds differently based on age, metabolism, and lifestyle, most real AquaSculpt users report noticeable changes within the first 7-10 days, with more transformative effects emerging after 30 to 60 days of consistent use.

First 7 Days: Metabolic Activation Phase

During the first week of taking AquaSculpt, the body begins absorbing and adapting to its nutrient-rich formula. This includes natural metabolism boosters, thermogenic enhancers like Fucoxanthin, and blood sugar regulators such as Berberine and Chromium. Early effects most frequently noted by verified buyers include:

  • Reduced appetite, particularly sugar cravings

  • More stable energy throughout the day (without caffeine spikes)

Importantly, AquaSculpt doesn't rely on harsh stimulants. Instead, it works in sync with your body's own fat-burning mechanisms, which is why users often describe their results as "subtle at first, but steady."

By 30 Days: Visible Fat Loss, Less Inflammation

The 30-day mark is where AquaSculpt reviews start to get exciting. Most users describe visible reductions in stubborn belly fat, especially around the midsection and hips. This is likely due to:

  • Improved insulin sensitivity leading to less fat storage

  • Reductions in cortisol-driven fat retention, especially for stressed or over-40 users

Real AquaSculpt consumer reports often mention slimmer waistlines, less bloating, and easier digestion around this time-many crediting the product with helping them break long-standing weight loss plateaus.

By 60 Days: Deeper Results & Body Recomposition

For users who continue using AquaSculpt for 60 days or more, the transformation can go beyond the scale. Some report:

  • More defined muscle tone (especially when combined with light activity)

  • Less yo-yo dieting behavior due to reduced appetite swings

  • Long-term stabilization of blood sugar, sleep, and digestive health

These outcomes make sense when you consider how AquaSculpt is designed not just to burn fat, but also to balance metabolic processes and hormonal pathways that influence weight gain.

Unlike typical fat burners that spike energy and crash, AquaSculpt's non-stimulant, cold-activation formula supports long-term use-meaning users often stick with it longer, and see compounding benefits.

Bottom line: Most people don't need to wait months to feel AquaSculpt working. You'll likely notice changes within a week, and continued use yields deeper, more sustainable weight loss that aligns with realistic biology-not fantasy marketing.

AquaSculpt vs Traditional Weight Loss Fat Burner Recipes and Formulas: What Makes This Ice Hack Different?

At first glance, AquaSculpt may sound like just another diet supplement in a crowded space-but its entire mechanism of action is radically different from typical fat burners.

Let's start with the most obvious contrast: stimulants.

Traditional Fat Burners: Short-Term, Jittery Results

Most commercial fat burners on the market are loaded with high doses of caffeine, yohimbine, or synephrine-compounds that force your central nervous system into overdrive, increasing your heart rate and thermogenesis artificially. These supplements can deliver fast water weight loss and slight calorie burns, but they come with major tradeoffs:

  • Anxiety and mood crashes

  • Sleep disruptions

  • Adrenal burnout

  • Rapid tolerance buildup (they stop working after a few weeks)

More concerning is the safety profile of these stimulant-based formulas, especially for older users or those with heart conditions. That's why many people search for non-stimulant alternatives-enter AquaSculpt.

AquaSculpt: Metabolic Activation via Cold Thermogenesis

Rather than force your metabolism with chemical stimulation, AquaSculpt works by activating the body's cold-response systems, mimicking what happens during cold exposure or "ice plunges." This triggers:

  • Brown adipose tissue (BAT) activation

  • AMPK pathway stimulation (the fat-burning master switch)

  • A boost in metabolic flexibility and mitochondrial efficiency

AquaSculpt's formula also includes ingredients that regulate insulin and leptin, making it easier for your body to burn fat efficiently and resist fat gain-especially in hormonal or age-related weight scenarios.

Results That Last vs Quick Fixes

While stimulant burners may give users a short-term burst of energy and slight water weight loss, AquaSculpt is designed for metabolic repair and sustainable fat burn. That means fewer rebounds, less cortisol-related belly fat, and long-term appetite control.

In side-by-side comparisons, AquaSculpt wins on tolerability, consistency, and long-term impact. It may not give you a buzz-but it will help you lose fat in a way that's gentle, natural, and repeatable.

Does AquaSculpt Actually Burn Fat or Just Help With Bloating and Water Weight?

Let's cut through the hype. A lot of weight loss supplements promise to melt fat but end up just shedding water weight for a few days-leading to disappointment and eventual rebound. So, what exactly does AquaSculpt do?

AquaSculpt Does Help With Bloating-But It Doesn't Stop There

Yes, AquaSculpt does help flush out retained water, especially in the first 7-10 days. This can lead to a quick drop in weight, reduced puffiness, and slimmer waistlines. But calling it a water pill would be a massive understatement.

What makes AquaSculpt different is its metabolic targeting-specifically via the cold-exposure activation pathway. This triggers a process known as non-shivering thermogenesis, which forces the body to generate heat by burning stored fat, not just water.

How AquaSculpt Burns Fat Over Time

Unlike most fat burners that rely on stimulants like caffeine or yohimbine, AquaSculpt uses:

  • Brown fat activation to increase daily calorie expenditure

  • Berberine, Chromium, and B6 to enhance insulin sensitivity

  • Fucoxanthin and L-Carnitine to accelerate fat metabolism gently

The result? A progressive fat-burning response that feels sustainable, especially for those who've struggled with age-related weight gain, hormonal shifts, or past crash diets.

>>Read Real 2025 Reviews, Watch Video Testimonials, And Decide For Yourself

It's a Full-System Reset, Not Just a Flush

Most AquaSculpt reviews describe an unexpected energy lift, better sleep, and fewer cravings. That's because the formula goes beyond aesthetics-it targets:

  • Adrenal health

  • Stress eating

So yes, AquaSculpt helps with bloating and water retention, but that's just Phase One. What follows is a genuine metabolic reset that helps your body shed real fat, and keep it off.

If you want a pill that bloats you down and rebounds later, you have thousands to choose from. But if you want one that burns actual fat through real pathways, AquaSculpt may be your smartest move.

Is AquaSculpt FDA-Approved? What the Manufacturing and Testing Process Looks Like

One of the most common questions buyers ask before hitting "Add to Cart" is whether AquaSculpt is FDA-approved. It's a fair question, especially in a market flooded with low-quality diet pills and wellness scams. But to answer it properly, we need to break down a key distinction: the difference between FDA approval and GMP certification.

FDA-Approved vs. FDA-Registered: What AquaSculpt Actually Is

To set the record straight, dietary supplements like AquaSculpt do not require FDA approval to be sold in the U.S. The FDA only approves drugs and medical devices, not supplements. So if any fat burner claims to be "FDA approved," that's a red flag.

What AquaSculpt does have, however, is FDA-registered manufacturingandGMP (Good Manufacturing Practice) certification, and that's where the trust lies.

AquaSculpt Is Manufactured in an FDA-Registered, GMP-Certified Facility

The creators of AquaSculpt have confirmed that the product is made in the United States in a facility that is both:

  • FDA-registered - meaning it complies with FDA manufacturing regulations

  • cGMP-certified - ensuring every batch meets strict purity, consistency, and safety guidelines

That means AquaSculpt undergoes routine inspections, third-party testing, and meticulous quality control from raw ingredient sourcing to bottling.

It's manufactured under controlled climate conditions, using state-of-the-art equipment and documented Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs). Each capsule is produced in accordance with 21 CFR Part 111, the FDA's official regulation for dietary supplements.

Ingredient-Level Safety Testing

Every compound inside AquaSculpt - including Berberine, Chromium, L-Carnitine, and Brown Algae Extract-is independently tested for:

  • Identity verification

  • Purity and potency

  • Microbial contamination

  • Heavy metals and solvent residues

You're not getting a back-alley blend of mystery herbs here. AquaSculpt follows pharmaceutical-grade protocols for every bottle-and that's something very few weight loss supplements can honestly claim.

So while AquaSculpt isn't FDA-approved (because no supplement is), it is produced with the highest level of transparency and safety oversight currently possible in the supplement industry.

A lot of supplements claim to offer "no-risk" guarantees-but how many actually follow through without red tape or customer support nightmares?

In the case of AquaSculpt, the refund policy has been one of the biggest reasons customers say they were confident giving it a try-and it's not just a marketing gimmick. The 180-day money-back guarantee is real, accessible, and extremely generous by industry standards.

The company has clearly invested in customer trust and long-term satisfaction, which is rare in this space. Their refund policy reflects that.

If you're the type of buyer who hesitates because "What if it doesn't work for me?"-this refund setup means you have zero risk trying AquaSculpt. The only real loss would be not testing it when your body might be ready for that change.

After digging through verified AquaSculpt reviews, analyzing the scientific principles behind cold-triggered thermogenesis, reviewing the clinical-grade formulation, and comparing it to both mainstream fat burners and fad diets, the final verdict is in:

AquaSculpt is not just another trendy bottle-it's one of the most grounded, low-risk weight support solutions we've seen in 2025.

data.title: IRBT Investors Have Opportunity to Lead iRobot Corporation Securities Fraud Lawsuit
data.description:
IRBT Investors Have Opportunity to Lead iRobot Corporation Securities Fraud Lawsuit

IRBT Investors Have Opportunity to Lead iRobot Corporation Securities Fraud Lawsuit

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, July 19, 2025

NEW YORK, July 19, 2025 /PRNewswire/ --

Why: Rosen Law Firm, a global investor rights law firm, reminds purchasers of securities of iRobot Corporation (NASDAQ: IRBT) between January 29, 2024 and March 11, 2025, both dates inclusive (the "Class Period"), of the important September 5, 2025 lead plaintiff deadline.

So What: If you purchased iRobot securities during the Class Period you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement.

What to do next: To join the iRobot class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=23275 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for more information. A class action lawsuit has already been filed. If you wish to serve as lead plaintiff, you must move the Court no later than September 5, 2025. A lead plaintiff is a representative party acting on behalf of other class members in directing the litigation.

Why Rosen Law: We encourage investors to select qualified counsel with a track record of success in leadership roles. Often, firms issuing notices do not have comparable experience, resources, or any meaningful peer recognition. Many of these firms do not actually litigate securities class actions, but are merely middlemen that refer clients or partner with law firms that actually litigate the cases. Be wise in selecting counsel. The Rosen Law Firm represents investors throughout the globe, concentrating its practice in securities class actions and shareholder derivative litigation. Rosen Law Firm achieved the largest ever securities class action settlement against a Chinese Company at the time. Rosen Law Firm was Ranked No. 1 by ISS Securities Class Action Services for number of securities class action settlements in 2017. The firm has been ranked in the top 4 each year since 2013 and has recovered hundreds of millions of dollars for investors. In 2019 alone the firm secured over $438 million for investors. In 2020, founding partner Laurence Rosen was named by law360 as a Titan of Plaintiffs' Bar. Many of the firm's attorneys have been recognized by Lawdragon and Super Lawyers.

Details of the case: According to the lawsuit, throughout the Class Period, defendants made false and misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (1) iRobot overstated the extent to which the Restructuring Plan would help iRobot maintain stability after the termination of the Amazon Acquisition; (2) as a result, it was unlikely that iRobot would be able to profitably operate as a standalone company; (3) accordingly, there was substantial doubt about iRobot's ability to continue as a going concern; and (4) as a result, defendants' public statements were materially false and misleading at all relevant times. When the true details entered the market, the lawsuit claims that investors suffered damages.

To join the iRobot class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=23275 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for more information.

No Class Has Been Certified. Until a class is certified, you are not represented by counsel unless you retain one. You may select counsel of your choice. data.title: CENTENE ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Urges Investors in Centene Corporation (CNC) to Inquire About Their Rights in Class Action Lawsuit
data.description:
Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Litigation Partner Brandon Walker Encourages Investors Who Suffered Losses In Centene Corporation (CNC) To Contact Him Directly To Discuss Their Options

If you purchased or acquired securities in Centene between December 12, 2024 to June 30, 2025 and would like to discuss your legal rights, call Bragar Eagel & Squire partner Brandon Walker or Marion Passmore directly at (212) 355-4648.

NEW YORK, July 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C., a nationally recognized stockholder rights law firm, announces that a class action lawsuit has been filed against Centene Corporation (“Centene” or the “Company”) (NYSE:CNC) in the United States District Court for the Southern District of New York on behalf of all persons and entities who purchased or otherwise acquired Centene securities between December 12, 2024 to June 30, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”). Investors have until September 8, 2025 to apply to the Court to be appointed as lead plaintiff in the lawsuit.

Click here to participate in the action.

According to the complaint, defendants created the false impression that they possessed reliable information pertaining to the Company’s projected revenue outlook and anticipated growth while also touting enrollment rates and low morbidity. In truth, Centene’s optimistic reports and promises regarding the Company’s inflated guidance fell short of reality when a preliminary analysis of over two-thirds of Centene’s marketplace share showed lower-than-anticipated enrollment and increased aggregate market morbidity.

Plaintiff alleges that on July 1, 2025, Centene issued a press release withdrawing 2025 guidance. Specifically, following an analysis of the 2025 Health Insurance Marketplace, Centene’s overall market growth across 22 states, or 72% of the Company’s marketplace membership, was lower than expected. In pertinent part, the Company stated that this preliminary analysis resulted in a reduction of its previously issued guidance to approximately $1.8 billion or an adjusted diluted EPS of $2.75. On this news, Centene's common stock fell from %56.65 per share on July 1, 2025, to $44.78 per share on July 2, 2025, a decline of over 40%.

If you purchased or otherwise acquired Centene shares and suffered a loss, are a long-term stockholder, have information, would like to learn more about these claims, or have any questions concerning this announcement or your rights or interests with respect to these matters, please contact Brandon Walker or Marion Passmore by email at investigations@bespc.com, telephone at (212) 355-4648, or by filling out this contact form. There is no cost or obligation to you.

About Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C.:

Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. is a nationally recognized law firm with offices in New York, California, and South Carolina. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in commercial, securities, derivative, and other complex litigation in state and federal courts across the country. For more information about the firm, please visit www.bespc.com. Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

SO WHAT: If you purchased National Grid securities you may be entitled to compensation without payment of any out of pocket fees or costs through a contingency fee arrangement. The Rosen Law Firm is preparing a class action seeking recovery of investor losses.

WHAT TO DO NEXT: To join the prospective class action, go to https://rosenlegal.com/submit-form/?case_id=41344 or call Phillip Kim, Esq. toll-free at 866-767-3653 or email case@rosenlegal.com for information on the class action.

WHAT IS THIS ABOUT: On July 2, 2025, Reuters published an article entitled “‘Preventable’ National Grid failures led to Heathrow fire, findings say.” The article stated that a “fire that shut London’s Heathrow airport in March, stranding thousands of people, was caused by the UK power grid’s failure to maintain an electricity substation, an official report said on Wednesday, prompting the energy watchdog to open a probe.” Further, the article stated that the United Kingdom’s Energy minister, Ed Miliband, had “called the report “deeply concerning”, after it concluded that the issue which caused the fire was identified seven years ago but went unaddressed by power grid operator National Grid[.]”

On this news, the price of National Grid American Depositary Shares (“ADSs”) fell 5%, on July 2, 2024.

Now available on both web and mobile devices, these flexible, short-term contracts allow users to mine BTC anywhere and receive daily payouts—no mining rigs, no complexity, and no prior experience needed.
For the first time, everyday users can engage directly with the Bitcoin economy through a streamlined, fully integrated, app-based platform.
Explore the PFMCrypto website or download the app today.

BTC Cloud Mining Is Here—Simple, Smart, and Mobile-Ready.
Long hailed as the original and most decentralized cryptocurrency, Bitcoin now enters a new era with PFMCrypto’s intuitive cloud mining service. Users can mine BTC directly or let PFMCrypto’s smart AI engine optimize their earnings by automatically shifting mining power to top-performing coins—such as ETH, XRP, DOGE, USDC, and more.
All earnings are paid out daily in the cryptocurrency of your choice, offering predictable income even during volatile markets. Whether you’re a beginner or an experienced investor, this platform lets you mine and earn—anytime, anywhere.

Key Features of PFMCrypto’s BTC Cloud Mining Contracts:
-  Full BTC Integration: Deposit, purchase, mine, and withdraw BTC directly within the platform.
-  Multi-Coin Mining Support: Choose to mine and receive payouts in ETH, XRP, DOGE, USDC, USDT, SOL, LTC, and BCH.
-  AI Revenue Optimization: Proprietary algorithms automatically allocate mining power to maximize returns.
-  100% Remote Access: No mining equipment or setup required—just use the PFMCrypto app or browser.
-  Capital Protection: Full principal return at contract maturity helps reduce risk while growing your assets.

Mining Contracts for Every Budget and Strategy:
PFMCrypto offers a wide selection of BTC-based mining contracts, catering to different investment levels and earning strategies. Every plan is designed for flexibility, low risk, and consistent income:
$10 Contract – 1 Day – Earn $0.66 (Free with sign-up bonus)

$500 Contract – 5 Days – Earn $6.15/day
$5,000 Contract – 30 Days – Earn $78.50/day
$20,000 Contract – 45 Days – Earn $380.00/day
Whether you're starting small or building a long-term portfolio, PFMCrypto delivers transparency and daily earnings in BTC.
Click here to explore more BTC cloud contracts.

Why PFMCrypto’s BTC Mining Stands Out?
-  Truly Accessible: No hardware, no setup—just log in and start earning.
-  BTC-Native Platform: Deposit, mine, and withdraw BTC in one seamless system.
-  Consistent, Optimized Returns: AI-driven strategies adjust in real time for maximum efficiency.
-  Multi-Asset Flexibility: Choose to mine BTC or diversify into other top crypto assets—all under one contract.
-  Instant, Global Access: Mine securely from anywhere via mobile app or web browser.

Get Started in 3 Easy Steps:
1.  Sign Up – Create an account and receive a $10 bonus instantly.
2.  Choose a Plan – Select a contract from 1 to 60 days.
3.  Start Earning – Track your daily profits and withdraw in BTC or your chosen token.

Start mining BTC today at: https://pfmcrypto.net 
Or download the PFMCrypto app (Available for iOS & Android)

BTC Mining for the Mobile-First Future:

Since 2018, PFMCrypto has helped millions of users worldwide generate passive crypto income through secure, cloud-based mining. Now, with the introduction of mobile BTC mining, the platform bridges the gap between retail accessibility and institutional-grade infrastructure.Users can earn directly in BTC or diversify into other leading digital assets—all from the palm of their hand, within a secure and fully remote environment.
“Bitcoin has always been secure, decentralized, and trusted globally,” said a PFMCrypto spokesperson. “Now, it’s also mobile-friendly, mineable, and profitable. We’ve removed the technical barriers—so anyone can be part of Bitcoin’s future.”
Markets may fluctuate—but your daily mining income doesn’t have to.

Join the BTC mining revolution now at: https://pfmcrypto.net

data.title: Stephen Colbert got tangled in the 'Trump transaction tax' - and it affects all investors
data.description:
By Blair Levin

Legal settlements like Paramount's with Trump erode shareholder value, this former FCC official says

M&A deals face new hurdles in the form of a 'Trump transaction tax' and a 'Trump transaction trap.'

Stephen Colbert, the CBS late-night talk show host, was unequivocal in his assessment of Paramount Global's $16 million settlement with President Donald Trump. "I believe this kind of complicated financial settlement with a sitting government official has a technical name in legal circles," he said. "It's a big fat bribe."

Jon Stewart of Comedy Central's "The Daily Show" followed suit, calling the settlement "shameful." Both hosts, who work for Paramount Global (PARA), the parent company of both CBS and Comedy Central, were nominated for Primetime Emmy Awards this week.

Colbert noted on Tuesday that the trade press was speculating that "the new owner's desire to please Trump could put pressure on late-night host and frequent Trump critic Stephen Colbert."

Two days later, CBS announced that "The Late Show with Stephen Colbert" would air its last show in May 2026. According to news reports on Thursday, Paramount and CBS said the cancellation "is purely a financial decision against a challenging backdrop in late night. It is not related in any way to the show's performance, content or other matters happening at Paramount."

How the Trump transaction tax is a trap

Excitement after the 2024 election for an M&A boom has given way to a new reality, unexpected by investors and chief executives. Deals face new hurdles in the form of a "Trump transaction tax" and a "Trump transaction trap."

The tax is the cost of a corporate transaction approval outside of the normal parameters of competition or public-interest analysis.

The trap is the negative consequences that come from paying the tax.

The Paramount-Skydance Media merger Colbert referenced is a perfect example. Paramount paid Trump $16 million to settle a lawsuit about a "60 Minutes" segment - something observers believe was necessary for Paramount and Skydance to obtain FCC approval for the deal.

While $16 million may not be material in the context of the $8 billion deal, the trap is. By paying it, Paramount's board is open to potential bribery charges, and/or the possibility of derivative shareholder suits.

Shareholders could claim that settling the lawsuit, widely perceived to be frivolous, was a waste of corporate assets. But a possible defense - that it was necessary to obtain deal approval - may be worse: providing something of value to a public official with the intent to influence their regulatory oversight is the very definition of a bribe.

Read: Paramount did what companies are supposed to do in complex legal terrain

The payment will make it harder and more expensive for Paramount to win projects.

There's another, less understood, tax as well. Media properties compete for two things: customers and talent. It is bad enough that the merger review eroded the value of both "60 Minutes" and CBS News, as well as causing the departure of top talent - now including Colbert.

But the bigger difficulty will be with the Paramount streaming service, perhaps the most valuable service going forward. The payment will make it harder and more expensive for Paramount to win projects they might want if the producers, directors, writers and actors have concerns about the willingness of management to withstand controversy about a program.

Further, it demonstrates to investors that streaming companies with federally regulated components are at a disadvantage relative to unregulated streaming companies such as YouTube (GOOG) (GOOGL), Apple+ (AAPL) and Netflix (NFLX).

Pay to play

The Trump transaction tax has been imposed on other media deals too. The Federal Trade Commission just approved advertising giant Omnicom Group's (OMC) acquisition of Interpublic Group (IPG)- but only after Omnicom agreed that it won't direct advertising away from media outlets based on political or ideological viewpoints. This condition appears designed to specifically benefit Trump's Truth Social and Elon Musk's X.

Omnicom is trapped. It cannot advise its clients to direct ads to the most beneficial platforms, but rather to use platforms that may be contrary to the clients' interests. Will this devalue the effectiveness of a $250 million global advertising budget that Omnicom has in its portfolio? Might an advertiser want to work with another agency that was not constrained in this manner? Yes, and yes.

The trap isn't limited to the federal government. States also have a role in approving corporate transactions and might look at issues differently from Washington. Verizon Communications (VZ), for example, had to agree to disown its DEI program to gain FCC approval to purchase Frontier Communications (FYBR). Verizon now faces increased scrutiny from the California Public Utilities Commission about whether its concessions to the FCC violate California law that requires supplier diversity. The tension between state and federal regulators represents an unresolved risk to the transaction.

Tough road for Disney and Comcast

The Trump transaction tax distorts company valuations and market forces that should be driving the deals.

The next iteration of the tax/trap will play out when the FCC loosens its broadcast-ownership rules, setting off a frenzy of broadcast transactions. Companies viewed as "Trump-friendly," such as Nexstar Media Group (NXST) and Sinclair (SBGI), seem positioned for a relatively smooth path for transactions - though past loyalty to Trump is no guarantee of an easy path.

A big problem awaits Walt Disney (DIS) and Comcast (CMCSA), whose pursuit of shareholder value may include buying or selling broadcast outlets. Trump regards these two media giants as enemies, arguing that their news coverage should cause them to lose their licenses.

Expect the FCC, as it did with Paramount, to raise questions and delay approval, making Disney and Comcast less attractive as buyers, even if strategically they are the best fit. Licenses they want to sell will be less attractive due to the kind of uncertainty and delays seen in the Paramount-Skydance transaction. Either way, the Trump transaction tax distorts company valuations and market forces that should be driving the deals.

This is the pain point for investors and management: fealty to free markets or fealty to Trump. The cost is likely higher than investors currently understand.

Blair Levin is the policy adviser to New Street Research, an equity research firm. Previously, Blair served as chief of staff to FCC Chairman Reed Hundt (1993-1997) and directed the writing of the United States National Broadband Plan (2009-2010).

More: Stephen Colbert, an outspoken Trump critic, says CBS is canceling his 'Late Show' in May 2026

Also read: Paramount's settlement with Trump could finally lead to its $8 billion Skydance merger

-Blair Levin

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  07-19-25 1117ET


Copyright (c) 2025 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: UROGEN ALERT: Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Urges Investors in UroGen Pharma (URGN) to Inquire About Their Rights in Class Action Lawsuit
data.description:
Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. Litigation Partner Brandon Walker Encourages Investors Who Suffered Losses In UroGen Pharma (URGN) To Contact Him Directly To Discuss Their Options

If you purchased or acquired securities in UroGen between July 27, 2023 and May 15, 2025 and would like to discuss your legal rights, call Bragar Eagel & Squire partner Brandon Walker or Marion Passmore directly at (212) 355-4648.

NEW YORK, July 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C., a nationally recognized stockholder rights law firm, announces that a class action lawsuit has been filed against UroGen Pharma Ltd. (“UroGen” or the “Company”) (NASDAQ:URGN) in the United States District Court for the District of New Jersey on behalf of all persons and entities who purchased or otherwise acquired UroGen securities between July 27, 2023 and May 15, 2025, both dates inclusive (the “Class Period”). Investors have until July 28, 2025 to apply to the Court to be appointed as lead plaintiff in the lawsuit.

Click here to participate in the action.

UroGen engages in the development and commercialization of solutions for specialty cancers. According to the complaint, UroGen's lead pipeline product is UGN-102 (mitomycin), an intravesical solution intended to treat low-grade intermediate risk non-muscle invasive bladder cancer.

The UroGen class action lawsuit alleges that defendants throughout the Class Period made false and/or misleading statements and/or failed to disclose that: (i) UroGen's ENVISION clinical study for UGN-102 was not designed to demonstrate substantial evidence of effectiveness of UGN-102 because it lacked a concurrent control arm; (ii) as a result, UroGen would have difficulty demonstrating that the duration of response endpoint was attributable to UGN-102; (iii) UroGen failed to heed the U.S. Food and Drug Administration's ("FDA") warnings about the study design used to support a new drug application ("NDA") for UGN-102; and (iv) as a result, there was a substantial risk that the NDA for UGN-102 would not be approved.

The UroGen class action lawsuit further alleges that on May 16, 2025, the FDA published a briefing document in advance of its Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee meeting regarding UroGen's NDA for UGN-102, which stated that "[g]iven that ENVISION lacked a concurrent control arm, the primary endpoints of complete response (CR) and duration of response (DOR) are difficult to interpret," and that the FDA had "recommended a randomized trial design to the Applicant several times during their product's development due to concerns with interpreting efficacy results" but UroGen "chose not to conduct a randomized trial with a design and endpoints that the FDA considered appropriate." On this news, the price of UroGen stock fell nearly 26%, according to the complaint.

Then, on May 21, 2025, the UroGen class action lawsuit further alleges that the Oncologic Drugs Advisory Committee voted against approving the UGN-102 NDA, finding that the overall benefit-risk of the investigation therapy UGN-102 is not favorable in patients with recurrent low-grade, intermediate-risk non-muscle invasive bladder cancer. On this news, the price of UroGen stock fell nearly 45%, according to the complaint.

If you purchased or otherwise acquired UroGen shares and suffered a loss, are a long-term stockholder, have information, would like to learn more about these claims, or have any questions concerning this announcement or your rights or interests with respect to these matters, please contact Brandon Walker or Marion Passmore by email at investigations@bespc.com, telephone at (212) 355-4648, or by filling out this contact form. There is no cost or obligation to you.

About Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C.:

Bragar Eagel & Squire, P.C. is a nationally recognized law firm with offices in New York, California, and South Carolina. The firm represents individual and institutional investors in commercial, securities, derivative, and other complex litigation in state and federal courts across the country. For more information about the firm, please visit www.bespc.com. Attorney advertising. Prior results do not guarantee similar outcomes.

data.title: XRP Whales Shift Funds Into VNBTC Cloud Mining Platform For Stable Passive Income
data.description:
LONDON, July 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Over the past two weeks, something unusual has been happening beneath the surface of the crypto markets: large XRP holders – often referred to as “whales” – have started moving vast amounts of their tokens into the cloud mining platform.

According to internal data from VNBTC, the platform has seen a sharp rise in XRP funding activity. It isn’t just a handful of users either. Several high-volume wallets have shifted their XRP into VNBTC’s system, suggesting a calculated pivot toward passive yield strategies.

This trend coincides with a broader spike in XRP interest. Prices are up over 36% this week, including a 6.7% bump in just the past 24 hours. Some traders are eyeing a major breakout, but others – especially long-term holders – appear more focused on turning their idle crypto into a source of consistent income.

VNBTC seems to be offering just that. The company lets users rent mining power remotely, allowing even non-technical investors to earn daily profits from Bitcoin, Dogecoin, and Litecoin mining. One XRP whale reportedly purchased multiple high-tier contracts within 48 hours of the platform's XRP integration update.

The system is simple: pick a mining plan, pay with XRP, and the mining starts automatically. Users can track profits daily and choose to withdraw or reinvest. Contract prices vary — some start at just $100, while others aimed at heavy investors go well above $70,000. Daily returns typically range from 2% to 10%, though results depend on market performance and the plan chosen.

How to Start Earning Crypto Profits On VNBTC?

  1. Go to the official website and click Register to create a VNBTC account
  2. Receive a $79 bonus
  3. Choose a preferred cloud mining contract(s) and purchase
  4. Automated mining begins with daily crypto profits reflecting every 24 hours
  5. Withdraw returns in a cryptocurrency of your choice, within the VNBTC-supported cryptocurrencies.
  6. Optionally, reinvest for more returns

Note, all initial investments can be withdrawn together with profits at the end of the contract duration.

About VNBTC

VNBTC is currently the top cloud mining platform that uses AI and emphasises green mining. With 200+ eco-friendly mining farms, VNBTC serves over 25 million investors offering stable passive income. This platform, launched in 2019, acquired FCA certification and continues to operate legally across 110 countries.

Media Contact:

James Carter
Marketing Specialist, VNBTC
James.Carter@vnbtc.com

data.title: SHAREHOLDER INVESTIGATION: Halper Sadeh LLC Investigates VRNA, DALN, RGLD on Behalf of Shareholders
data.description:
NEW YORK, July 19, 2025 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm, is investigating the following companies for potential violations of the federal securities laws and/or breaches of fiduciary duties to shareholders relating to:

Verona Pharma plc (NASDAQ: VRNA)’s sale to Merck for $107.00 per American Depository Share. If you are a Verona shareholder, click here to learn more about your legal rights and options.

DallasNews Corporation (NASDAQ: DALN)’s sale to Hearst for $14.00 in cash per share. If you are a DallasNews shareholder, click here to learn more about your rights and options.

Royal Gold Inc. (NASDAQ: RGLD)’s merger with Sandstorm Gold Ltd. Upon completion of the proposed transaction, existing Royal Gold shareholders will own approximately 77% of the combined company. If you are a Royal Gold shareholder, click here to learn more about your legal rights and options.

Halper Sadeh LLC may seek increased consideration for shareholders, additional disclosures and information concerning the proposed transaction, or other relief and benefits on behalf of shareholders. We would handle the action on a contingent fee basis, whereby you would not be responsible for out-of-pocket payment of our legal fees or expenses.

Shareholders are encouraged to contact the firm free of charge to discuss their legal rights and options. Please call Daniel Sadeh or Zachary Halper at (212) 763-0060 or email sadeh@halpersadeh.com or zhalper@halpersadeh.com.

Halper Sadeh LLC represents investors all over the world who have fallen victim to securities fraud and corporate misconduct. Our attorneys have been instrumental in implementing corporate reforms and recovering millions of dollars on behalf of defrauded investors.

Attorney Advertising. Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome.
